skill.name: claude-code-hooks
skill.description: Guidance for configuring Claude Code hooks, matchers, event handlers, audit logging, and security controls for hook scripts.
skill.summary: Configure Claude Code hooks for validation, logging, approvals, and session automation.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, environment, and network indicators. Most are documentation examples for Claude Code hooks, but the skill teaches arbitrary hook command execution and includes logging examples that may capture sensitive tool input.

medium_findings.title: Hook Examples Execute Local Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill shows Claude Code hooks that run local shell scripts before or after tool use. This is intended, but unsafe scripts can block actions, modify files, or execute attacker-controlled commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly configures command hooks. The risk is inherent to the feature, not evidence of malicious bundled code.

medium_findings.title: Audit Examples May Log Sensitive Tool Input
medium_findings.description: Some examples append full hook input or tool input to audit logs. Tool input can include prompts, shell commands, file paths, or file content that should be redacted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited examples log structured hook input. Claude Code tool inputs may contain sensitive data, so this is a concrete documentation risk.

medium_findings.title: Environment Loading Can Expose Local Secrets
medium_findings.description: The SessionStart example sources a project environment file, and another example uses an API key in a curl command. Users must restrict which hooks can read and transmit secrets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples intentionally access environment data. There is no malicious endpoint, but hook scripts could misuse inherited secrets.

low_findings.title: Hidden Claude Configuration Paths Are Expected
low_findings.description: References to Claude settings files are normal for hook configuration. They are filesystem-sensitive, but the skill does not instruct broad hidden-file harvesting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are standard Claude Code configuration locations. I found no evidence of unrelated hidden-file collection.

low_findings.title: Destructive Command Strings Are Deny-List Examples
low_findings.description: The destructive command strings appear inside a dangerous-command block list. They are examples of commands to reject, not commands to execute.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text says to block dangerous commands, and the loop exits when a pattern is found. This strongly supports false-positive classification.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to official Claude Code documentation. I found no evidence that the skill sends data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed as references under official documentation. They are not used in executable network code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unredacted Hook Input Logging
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples write hook input or tool input directly to log files. This pattern should be changed to log only sanitized metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible in documentation examples and can expose sensitive tool data. It is a documentation issue rather than bundled malicious code.

content.value_statement: Teams need predictable control over Claude Code tool execution and audit trails. This skill explains hook events, configuration patterns, and safer script practices for validation and logging.
actual_capabilities: Explains Claude Code hook events and when each event runs.
Shows settings examples for matchers, timeouts, and command hooks.
Provides shell patterns for parsing hook input and exit codes.
Documents validation patterns for paths, commands, and JSON input.
Covers audit logging, session hooks, and troubleshooting commands.
limitations: It is documentation and does not include ready-to-run hook scripts.
Examples require Claude Code settings support and local shell tools.
Security depends on each user-written hook script.
Some logging examples need redaction before production use.

use_cases.title: Block Risky Tool Actions
use_cases.description: Create PreToolUse hooks that reject dangerous shell commands or file paths before Claude Code runs them.

use_cases.title: Record Tool Activity
use_cases.description: Configure PostToolUse hooks that capture sanitized metadata for compliance, debugging, and operational reviews.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Hooks
use_cases.description: Define project-local hook settings so teams apply consistent validation and session setup across repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to explain how to add a simple Claude Code PreToolUse hook for Bash commands in one project.

prompt_templates.title: Design Safe Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a PostToolUse audit hook that logs tool names and timestamps without recording secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Review Hook Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Claude Code hook plan for command injection, path traversal, timeout, and secret-handling risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Hook Policy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a project hook policy for approvals, dangerous command blocking, audit logging, and session startup.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to block writes outside the project directory.
output_examples.output: The assistant describes a PreToolUse validation flow, required matcher, path checks, and exit behavior for blocking unsafe writes.

output_examples.input: A platform team wants an audit trail for Claude Code usage.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes sanitized metadata fields, log file placement, retention notes, and warnings against storing full tool input.

output_examples.input: A security reviewer asks whether hook scripts can access secrets.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains environment exposure, project file risks, network transmission controls, and script permission recommendations.

best_practices: Log sanitized metadata instead of full prompts, file contents, or command input.
Quote shell variables, validate JSON input, and restrict paths to the project directory.
Set short timeouts and use exit code 2 only for intentional blocking decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not source environment files unless the hook needs those variables.
Do not send hook input to external services without explicit user approval.
Do not use wildcard matchers for powerful hooks without clear scope controls.

faq.question: Does this skill install hooks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents how to configure hooks and write scripts for Claude Code.

faq.question: Which Claude Code events are covered?
faq.answer: It covers PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest, Notification, prompt, session, stop, and compaction events.

faq.question: Can hooks block a tool call?
faq.answer: Yes. PreToolUse hooks can block actions when a script exits with the documented blocking code.

faq.question: Is audit logging safe by default?
faq.answer: No. Audit hooks should redact prompts, file contents, commands, tokens, and environment values before logging.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: The content is Claude Code focused, but Codex users can use it for audit review and hook design guidance.

faq.question: What should I review before production use?
faq.answer: Review matchers, file permissions, path validation, secret handling, external network calls, and timeout settings.
